make.description: Allocates and initializes an object of type slice, map, or chan. For slices, the second argument is length and optional third is capacity. For maps, an optional size hint. For channels, an optional buffer capacity.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: The type to create (must be slice, map, or channel)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Length and optional capacity for slices; optional capacity for maps and channels

new.description: Allocates zeroed storage for a new item of the given type and returns a pointer to it.

append.description: Appends elements to the end of a slice. If the backing array has sufficient capacity, the destination is resliced. If not, a new array is allocated and the returned slice will point to the newly allocated array.

copy.description: Copies elements from src into dst. Returns the number of elements copied, which is the minimum of len(src) and len(dst). Also supports copying bytes from a string to []byte.

delete.description: Deletes the element with the specified key from the map. If the map is nil or there is no such element, delete is a no-op.

len.description: Returns the length of v: number of elements in array, slice, map, or channel buffer; number of bytes in a string.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: Array, slice, map, string, or channel

cap.description: Returns the capacity of v: the maximum length a slice can reach; the length of the underlying array; or the channel buffer capacity.

close.description: Closes the channel, indicating no more values will be sent. Only the sender should close a channel.

panic.description: Stops normal execution of the current goroutine. Deferred functions are still executed. Panics propagate up the call stack until recovered or the program crashes.

recover.description: Allows a program to manage behavior of a panicking goroutine. Only useful inside deferred functions. Returns the value that was passed to panic, or nil if the goroutine is not panicking.

complex.description: Constructs a complex value from two floating-point values.

real.description: Returns the real part of a complex number.

imag.description: Returns the imaginary part of a complex number.

clear.description: (Go 1.21+) For maps, deletes all entries. For slices, sets all elements up to the length to the zero value.

min.signature: func min[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
min.description: (Go 1.21+) Returns the smallest value of the fixed arguments. At least one argument is required.

max.signature: func max[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
max.description: (Go 1.21+) Returns the largest value of the fixed arguments. At least one argument is required.

print.description: Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way and writes to standard error. For production use, prefer the fmt package.

println.description: Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way and writes to standard error with spaces and a trailing newline.

error.description: The built-in error interface. The conventional interface for representing an error condition, with the nil value representing no error.

Error.description: Returns a string describing the error.

string.description: The set of all strings of 8-bit bytes, conventionally but not necessarily representing UTF-8 text. A string may be empty but not nil. Values of string type are immutable.

int.description: A signed integer type that is at least 32 bits in size. Platform-dependent: 32 bits on 32-bit systems, 64 bits on 64-bit systems.

float64.description: An IEEE-754 64-bit floating-point number. The default floating-point type in Go.

bool.description: The set of boolean values: true and false.

byte.description: An alias for uint8. Conventionally used to distinguish byte values from 8-bit unsigned integer values.

rune.description: An alias for int32. Represents a Unicode code point.

any.description: (Go 1.18+) An alias for interface{}. Can hold a value of any type.

comparable.description: (Go 1.18+) An interface implemented by all comparable types. Used as a type constraint in generic functions and types.

fmt.description: Implements formatted I/O with functions analogous to C's printf and scanf. The format 'verbs' are derived from C's but are simpler.

Println.description: Formats using default formats, writes to stdout, adds spaces and a newline.

Printf.signature: func Printf(format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
Printf.description: Formats according to a format specifier and writes to stdout.

Sprintf.description: Formats according to a format specifier and returns the resulting string.

Fprintf.signature: func Fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
Fprintf.description: Formats according to a format specifier and writes to w.

Errorf.description: Formats and returns an error. Use %w to wrap errors.

Sscanf.signature: func Sscanf(str string, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
Sscanf.description: Scans the argument string, storing values into successive arguments.

Stringer.description: Implemented by any value that has a String method, defining the native format for printing.

os.description: Provides a platform-independent interface to operating system functionality. The design is Unix-like, although the error handling is Go-like.

Open.description: Opens the named file for reading.

Create.description: Creates or truncates the named file.

ReadFile.description: Reads the named file and returns the contents.

WriteFile.signature: func WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm FileMode) error
WriteFile.description: Writes data to the named file, creating it if necessary.

Args.description: Command-line arguments, starting with the program name.

Exit.description: Causes the current program to exit with the given status code.

Getenv.description: Retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key.

Setenv.description: Sets the value of the environment variable named by the key.

MkdirAll.signature: func MkdirAll(path string, perm FileMode) error
MkdirAll.description: Creates a directory named path along with any necessary parents.

Remove.description: Removes the named file or empty directory.

io.description: Provides basic interfaces to I/O primitives. Its primary job is to wrap existing implementations of such primitives into shared public interfaces.

Reader.signature: type Reader interface { Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) }
Reader.description: The interface that wraps the basic Read method.

Writer.signature: type Writer interface { Write(p []byte) (n int, err error) }
Writer.description: The interface that wraps the basic Write method.

ReadCloser.description: Groups the basic Read and Close methods.

ReadAll.description: Reads from r until error or EOF and returns data.

Copy.signature: func Copy(dst Writer, src Reader) (written int64, err error)
Copy.description: Copies from src to dst until EOF.

NopCloser.description: Returns a ReadCloser with a no-op Close method wrapping the Reader.

EOF.description: The error returned by Read when no more input is available.

strings.description: Implements simple functions to manipulate UTF-8 encoded strings.

Contains.description: Reports whether substr is within s.

HasPrefix.description: Reports whether the string s begins with prefix.

HasSuffix.description: Reports whether the string s ends with suffix.

Split.description: Slices s into substrings separated by sep.

Join.description: Concatenates string slice elements with a separator.

TrimSpace.description: Returns a string with all leading and trailing white space removed.

Replace.signature: func Replace(s, old, new string, n int) string
Replace.description: Returns a copy of s with the first n non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new.

ReplaceAll.signature: func ReplaceAll(s, old, new string) string
ReplaceAll.description: Returns a copy of s with all non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new.

ToUpper.description: Returns a copy of s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case.

ToLower.description: Returns a copy of s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case.

Builder.description: A Builder is used to efficiently build a string using Write methods.

NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader reading from s.

strconv.description: Implements conversions to and from string representations of basic data types.

Atoi.description: Parses a string as a decimal integer.

Itoa.description: Returns the string representation of the integer i.

FormatFloat.signature: func FormatFloat(f float64, fmt byte, prec, bitSize int) string
FormatFloat.description: Converts the floating-point number f to a string.

ParseInt.signature: func ParseInt(s string, base int, bitSize int) (int64, error)
ParseInt.description: Interprets a string s in the given base and bit size.

ParseFloat.signature: func ParseFloat(s string, bitSize int) (float64, error)
ParseFloat.description: Converts the string s to a floating-point number.

FormatInt.signature: func FormatInt(i int64, base int) string
FormatInt.description: Returns the string representation of i in the given base.

ParseBool.description: Returns the boolean value represented by the string.

encoding/json.description: Implements encoding and decoding of JSON as defined in RFC 7159.

Marshal.description: Returns the JSON encoding of v.

MarshalIndent.signature: func MarshalIndent(v any, prefix, indent string) ([]byte, error)
MarshalIndent.description: Like Marshal but with indentation for readability.

Unmarshal.description: Parses the JSON-encoded data and stores the result in v.

NewEncoder.description: Returns a new encoder that writes to w.

NewDecoder.description: Returns a new decoder that reads from r.

RawMessage.description: A raw encoded JSON value. Can delay JSON decoding or precompute a JSON encoding.

Valid.description: Reports whether data is a valid JSON encoding.

net/http.description: Provides HTTP client and server implementations.

ListenAndServe.signature: func ListenAndServe(addr string, handler Handler) error
ListenAndServe.description: Listens on addr and serves requests using handler.

HandleFunc.description: Registers the handler function for the given pattern.

Get.description: Issues a GET to the specified URL.

Post.signature: func Post(url, contentType string, body io.Reader) (*Response, error)
Post.description: Issues a POST to the specified URL.

NewRequest.signature: func NewRequest(method, url string, body io.Reader) (*Request, error)
NewRequest.description: Returns a new Request given a method, URL, and optional body.

NewServeMux.description: Allocates and returns a new ServeMux.

ResponseWriter.signature: type ResponseWriter interface { Header() Header; Write([]byte) (int, error); WriteHeader(statusCode int) }
ResponseWriter.description: Interface used by HTTP handler to construct an HTTP response.

Request.signature: type Request struct { Method string; URL *url.URL; Header Header; Body io.ReadCloser; ... }
Request.description: Represents an HTTP request received by a server or to be sent by a client.

StatusInternalServerError.description: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error status code.

context.description: Defines the Context type, which carries deadlines, cancellation signals, and other request-scoped values across API boundaries and between processes.

Context.signature: type Context interface { Deadline() (time.Time, bool); Done() <-chan struct{}; Err() error; Value(key any) any }
Context.description: Carries a deadline, cancellation signal, and request-scoped values across API boundaries.

Background.description: Returns a non-nil, empty Context. Never canceled, no values, no deadline.

TODO.description: Returns a non-nil, empty Context for when it's unclear which Context to use.

WithCancel.signature: func WithCancel(parent Context) (ctx Context, cancel CancelFunc)
WithCancel.description: Returns a copy of parent with a new Done channel.

WithTimeout.signature: func WithTimeout(parent Context, timeout time.Duration) (Context, CancelFunc)

WithDeadline.signature: func WithDeadline(parent Context, d time.Time) (Context, CancelFunc)
WithDeadline.description: Returns a copy of parent with deadline adjusted to be no later than d.

WithValue.signature: func WithValue(parent Context, key, val any) Context
WithValue.description: Returns a copy of parent with key associated with val.

CancelFunc.description: Tells an operation to abandon its work. Does not wait for the work to stop.

sync.description: Provides basic synchronization primitives such as mutual exclusion locks. Other than Once and WaitGroup, most are intended for use by low-level library routines.

Mutex.description: A mutual exclusion lock. Zero value is an unlocked mutex.

RWMutex.description: A reader/writer mutual exclusion lock. Multiple readers or one writer.

WaitGroup.description: Waits for a collection of goroutines to finish. Call Add, Done, Wait.

Once.description: An object that will perform exactly one action. Call Do(f).

Map.description: A concurrent-safe map. Methods: Store, Load, Delete, Range.

Pool.signature: type Pool struct { New func() any }
Pool.description: A pool of temporary objects that may be individually saved and retrieved.

errors.description: Implements functions to manipulate errors. The New function creates errors whose only content is a text message. The Is, As, and Unwrap functions work on errors that may wrap other errors.

New.description: Returns an error that formats as the given text.

Is.description: Reports whether any error in err's tree matches target.

As.signature: func As(err error, target any) bool
As.description: Finds the first error in err's tree that matches target and sets target.

Unwrap.description: Returns the result of calling Unwrap on err.

Join.description: (Go 1.20+) Returns an error that wraps the given errors.

time.description: Provides functionality for measuring and displaying time. The calendrical calculations always assume a Gregorian calendar, with no leap seconds.

Since.description: Returns the time elapsed since t.

Sleep.description: Pauses the current goroutine for at least the duration d.

NewTicker.description: Returns a new Ticker that sends the time on its channel at each tick.

After.description: Waits for duration then sends current time on returned channel.

Parse.description: Parses a formatted string and returns the time value.

Duration.description: Represents elapsed time in nanoseconds between two instants.

Time.description: Represents an instant in time with nanosecond precision.

sort.description: Provides primitives for sorting slices and user-defined collections.

Slice.signature: func Slice(x any, less func(i, j int) bool)
Slice.description: Sorts the slice x using the provided less function.

SliceStable.signature: func SliceStable(x any, less func(i, j int) bool)
SliceStable.description: Sorts the slice x keeping equal elements in their original order.

Search.signature: func Search(n int, f func(int) bool) int
Search.description: Uses binary search to find the smallest index i in [0, n) at which f(i) is true.

Ints.description: Sorts a slice of ints in increasing order.

Strings.description: Sorts a slice of strings in increasing order.

slices.description: (Go 1.21+) Defines various functions useful with slices of any type. Unless otherwise specified, these functions all apply to the elements of a slice at index 0 <= i < len(s).

Sort.description: Sorts a slice of any ordered type in ascending order.

SortFunc.signature: func SortFunc[S ~[]E, E any](x S, cmp func(a, b E) int)
SortFunc.description: Sorts the slice x using the provided comparison function.

SortStableFunc.signature: func SortStableFunc[S ~[]E, E any](x S, cmp func(a, b E) int)
SortStableFunc.description: Sorts the slice x keeping equal elements in original order.

Contains.signature: func Contains[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S, v E) bool
Contains.description: Reports whether v is present in s.

Index.signature: func Index[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S, v E) int
Index.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of v, or -1.

Compact.signature: func Compact[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S) S
Compact.description: Replaces consecutive runs of equal elements with a single copy.

Reverse.description: Reverses the elements of the slice in place.

Min.signature: func Min[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S) E
Min.description: Returns the minimal value in x. Panics if x is empty.

Max.signature: func Max[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S) E
Max.description: Returns the maximal value in x. Panics if x is empty.

maps.description: (Go 1.21+) Defines various functions useful with maps of any type.

Keys.signature: func Keys[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) []K
Keys.description: Returns the keys of the map m in indeterminate order.

Values.signature: func Values[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) []V
Values.description: Returns the values of the map m in indeterminate order.

Clone.signature: func Clone[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) M
Clone.description: Returns a shallow copy of m.

Copy.signature: func Copy[M1 ~map[K]V, M2 ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](dst M1, src M2)
Copy.description: Copies all key/value pairs from src into dst.

DeleteFunc.signature: func DeleteFunc[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M, del func(K, V) bool)
DeleteFunc.description: Deletes entries from m for which del returns true.

Equal.signature: func Equal[M1 ~map[K]V, M2 ~map[K]V, K comparable, V comparable](m1 M1, m2 M2) bool
Equal.description: Reports whether two maps contain the same key/value pairs.

log/slog.description: (Go 1.21+) Provides structured logging, with a Logger type that produces log records containing a message, a severity level, and various other attributes.

Info.description: Logs at LevelInfo on the default logger.

Error.description: Logs at LevelError on the default logger.

Debug.description: Logs at LevelDebug on the default logger.

Warn.description: Logs at LevelWarn on the default logger.

With.description: Returns a Logger that includes given attributes in each output.

New.description: Creates a Logger that writes to h.

NewTextHandler.description: Creates a TextHandler that writes to w.

NewJSONHandler.description: Creates a JSONHandler that writes to w.

path/filepath.description: Implements utility routines for manipulating filename paths in a way compatible with the target operating system-defined file paths.

Join.description: Joins path elements into a single path with OS separator.

Dir.description: Returns all but the last element of path.

Base.description: Returns the last element of path.

Walk.signature: func Walk(root string, fn WalkFunc) error
Walk.description: Walks the file tree rooted at root, calling fn for each file or directory.

Glob.description: Returns the names of all files matching the pattern.

Abs.description: Returns the absolute representation of path.

regexp.description: Implements regular expression search. The syntax accepted is the same general syntax used by Perl, Python, and other languages (RE2 syntax).

Compile.description: Parses a regular expression and returns a Regexp object.

MustCompile.description: Like Compile but panics if the expression cannot be parsed.

MatchString.signature: func MatchString(pattern string, s string) (matched bool, err error)
MatchString.description: Reports whether the string s contains any match of the pattern.

log.description: Implements a simple logging package. It defines a type, Logger, with methods for formatting output.

Println.description: Calls Output to print to the standard logger with timestamp.

Printf.description: Calls fmt.Sprintf and prints to the standard logger.

Fatal.description: Equivalent to Println() followed by os.Exit(1).

Fatalf.description: Equivalent to Printf() followed by os.Exit(1).

SetOutput.description: Sets the output destination for the standard logger.

New.signature: func New(out io.Writer, prefix string, flag int) *Logger

bytes.description: Implements functions for the manipulation of byte slices. Analogous to the facilities of the strings package.

Buffer.description: A variable-sized buffer of bytes with Read and Write methods.

NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader reading from b.

Contains.description: Reports whether subslice is within b.

Equal.description: Reports whether a and b are the same length and contain the same bytes.

Split.description: Slices s into subslices separated by sep.

bufio.description: Implements buffered I/O. It wraps an io.Reader or io.Writer object, creating another object that also implements the interface but provides buffering and some help for textual I/O.

NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader whose buffer has the default size.

NewScanner.description: Returns a new Scanner to read from r.

NewWriter.description: Returns a new Writer whose buffer has the default size.

Scanner.description: Provides a convenient interface for reading data such as a file of newline-delimited lines.
